news.headline: Former President Donald Trump's crypto fortune includes over half a million TRUMP tokens. A bill allowing federal tax payments in Bitcoin may be introduced.
news.summary: This week has seen significant developments in the finance and cryptocurrency world as former President Donald Trump has surfaced with a potential crypto fortune. Trump's digital wallet contains more than 500,000 TRUMP tokens, which has stirred speculation about the total value of his holdings. The tokens, affiliated with MAGA, were distributed to Trump's wallet as part of a marketing strategy by the crypto platform. Additionally, there has been introduction of a bill that would permit federal tax payments to be made in Bitcoin. These events have kept the news cycle active and have garnered attention in the financial sector.

US banks increase dividends for Q3 after successful stress test; JPMorgan Chase raises dividend to $1.25/share, approves $30 billion in buybacks.
news.summary: Multiple major U.S. banks, including JPMorgan Chase & Co (NYSE: JPM), Bank of America Corp (NYSE: BAC), and Citigroup Inc (NYSE: C), have announced plans to increase their dividends for the third quarter after passing the Federal Reserve's stress test. Following the annual health assessment by the Fed, these banks demonstrated resilience against significant economic and market disruptions. JPMorgan Chase, the largest U.S. lender, raised its dividend from $1.15 to $1.25 per share and authorized $30 billion for new share repurchases starting July 1. Bank of America and Citigroup also raised their dividends to 26 cents and 56 cents per share, respectively. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: 'Rich Dad Poor Dad' author Robert Kiyosaki questions authenticity of Bitcoin ETFs, prefers physical gold, silver, and Bitcoin.
news.summary: Renowned author Robert Kiyosaki, famous for "Rich Dad Poor Dad," recently expressed skepticism regarding Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs). He shared his concerns about the authenticity of these financial instruments and indicated hesitancy towards investing in them. Drawing comparisons to gold and silver ETFs, Kiyosaki argued that they often do not accurately represent the underlying assets they claim to hold. Specifically, he pointed out the potential for gold ETFs to sell the same ounce of gold multiple times, which undermines their value as a substitute for physically owning gold. Therefore, Kiyosaki advocates for holding physical assets like gold, silver, and Bitcoin, stored securely and independently from traditional financial systems. Overall, he questions the reliability of ETFs and believes in the importance of direct ownership of assets.

news.headline: Ethereum's Dencun upgrade introduces blobs to boost scalability by enhancing data storage for cheaper and more efficient rollups.
news.summary: Recently integrated into Ethereum's Dencun upgrade, blobs are a cutting-edge data storage solution that enhances the cost-effectiveness and efficiency of rollups. This advancement has significantly streamlined transaction processing and is projected to reduce the gas fee per transaction by approximately 25%, translating to substantial savings for users. With this innovative feature, Ethereum users can expect smoother operations and increased scalability in their transactions.

news.headline: Kyowa Hakko Bio/Kirin Holdings will discuss citicoline benefits in aging at ASN's Annual Conference.

The program will highlight the potential benefits of citicoline in preventing age-related brain issues. This event is scheduled to take place in New York on June 29, 2024. This collaboration aims to shed light on the role of citicoline in promoting brain health, with a focus on addressing cognitive decline associated with aging.

news.headline: Launch of Ether ETF delayed by SEC comments on S-1 forms. Revisions needed by July 8, pushing potential launch to mid-to-late July.

news.headline: Blockchain company ConsenSys sued by SEC for unauthorized crypto transactions involving 36M assets through MetaMask.
news.summary: ConsenSys, a blockchain software company, is facing a lawsuit from the US SEC for alleged violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit pertains to unregistered securities offered through its MetaMask Swaps and Staking platforms. These platforms were involved in facilitating a total of 36 million crypto asset transactions. The SEC's legal action highlights the importance of compliance with securities regulations in the crypto industry.
